content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
Debra Gravert 
Chief Administrative Officer 
Assembly Rules Committee 
State Capitol 
P O Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 94249-0124 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-14-214 
Dear Ms. Gravert: 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
January 2, 2015 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the behested payment provisions 
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"J.1 
QUESTION 
Is free or bclov,•-markct rent provided by a private organization at the behest of an 
Assemblymemhcr considered a bchested payment on behalf of the Assemblymcmber? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. The free or discounted rem for an Assemblymcmber' s district office is a ··behested 
payment" to the Assemblymember if the payment is provided by a private organization, and not 
by a local, state. or federal government agency. A report detailing these payments must be filed 
by the official with your agency within 30 days after reaching the $5,000 threshold and each 30 
days thereafter for additional donations by the same donor. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
rdi:rences arc to the Go\'crnment Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission arc contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references arc to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless othern isc indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-14-214 
Page No. 2 
You are chief administrative officer of the California Assembly. You are in the process 
of arranging district office leases for 27 new Assemblymembers and some returning members. 
The Assembly has 80 Members, each of whom has one or more Assembly offices in his or her 
district. Each of these district offices is leased on behalf of the Assembly through the Assembly 
Rules Committee, which is the official lessee. The Rules Committee pays the monthly lease 
amount to the lessor. If the lease is for an Assemblymember's primary district office, the Rules 
Committee does not charge the lease amount to his or her office budget. If the lease is for a 
satellite district office, the lease amount is charged against the Assemblymember's office budget. 
Occasionally, a local, state or federal government agency or private organization may, at 
an Assemblymember's request, offer the Assembly the use of office space for the 
Assemblymember's primary district office, or for his or her satellite office, for free or at a 
minimal charge. If this occurs, the monthly lease amount paid by the Assembly may be 
significantly below the fair market rental value of the space. 
ANALYSIS 
Contributions and Behested Payments 
Section 82015 defines "contribution" as any payment, forgiveness of a loan, a payment of 
a loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment except to the extent that full 
and adequate consideration is received unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that 
it is not made for political purposes.2 
A "contribution" includes a payment made at the behest of a candidate. (Section 
82015(b).) A payment is made at the "behest" of a candidate whenever it is made "under the 
control or at the direction of, in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with, at the 
request or suggestion of, or with the express, prior consent of' a candidate or elected officer. 
(Section 82015; Regulation 18215.3.) A behested payment is made principally for legislative, 
governmental, or charitable purposes, and is neither a gift3 nor a contribution. (Section 
820 l 5(b)(2)(B )(iii).) 
A payment is not "made at the behest" of an elected officer under Section 
82015(b)(2)(B)(iii) and is not subject to reporting if the elected officer makes a request for a 
payment: 1) from a local, state, or federal governmental agency; and 2) that payment will be used 
2 A payment is made for political purposes if it is: ( 1) for the purpose of influencing or attempting 10 
influence lhe action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidale or candidates, or the 
qualificalion or passage of any measure; or (2) received by or made at the behest of a candidate. (Regulation 
I 8215(a).) 
3 A payment made principally for personal purposes may be considered a gift under lhe provisions of 
Seclion 82028. Payments that arc otherwise subject to the limits of Section 86203 arc presumed to be principally for 
personal purposes. (Section 82015(b)(2)(B)(i).) 


File No. A-14-214 
Page No. 3 
in the regular course of official agency business of the elected officer. ... " (Regulation 
l8215.3(c).) 
Your facts indicate that a third-party will offer free or discounted rent for an 
Assemblymember's district office at the behest of the Assembly member. This payment will be 
made to the Assembly Rules Committee, which is the official lessee of the property. The 
"payment" amount is the difference between the market value rent and the reduced rent paid by 
the Assembly.4 This payment is being made principally for a legislative or governmental 
purpose as the district office will be used by the Assemblymember to carry out official agency 
business and perform his or her official duties. 
Therefore, if the payment is from a local, state, or federal government agency, the 
payment is not "made at the behest" of the elected official and is not subject to reporting under 
Regulation 18215.3(c). 
If a private organization provides free or discounted rent for an Assemblymember's 
district office, the payment is considered a "behested payment" to the Assemblymember. A 
report detailing these payments must be filed with your agency within 30 days after reaching the 
$5,000 threshold and each 30 days thereafter for additional donations by the same donor. 
ER:vll 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
_
4
eneral Counsel 
t-..e_ _ 
-_c.£;:r- g_.15 -
( By: 
Emelyn Rodriguez 
U 
Counsel, Legal Division 
4 We note that you distinguished between an Assemhlymcmber's primary district office and an 
Assemblymember's "satellltc" district office. Because in both instances, public funds arc used to pay for lease of 
these offices, we do not distinguish between them.

by the official with your agency within 30 days after reaching the $5,000 threshold and each 30

The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission arc contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references arc to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless othern isc indicated.
